Title: Preparing a Balanced Corpus for Development of Turkish Speech Synthesis Systems
Notes: Single male speaker; Istanbul Turkish; fragile hosting on Google Drive; companion to P14

Title: Multilingual Speech Corpus for Low-Resource Turkic Languages
Notes: All target audio is synthetic (ElevenLabs, MMS, TurkicTTS, CoquiTTS); corpus closed pending copyright

Title: TatarTTS: Open-Source TTS Dataset for Tatar
Notes: OUT OF SCOPE: Tatar, not Turkish; retained only as the open-Turkic comparison and NOT counted in the 19 surveyed works. Gold standard for open Turkic TTS; template for Turkish work; 2 professional speakers

Title: Synthetic Data Generation for Turkish ASR
Notes: ASR paper, not TTS; behind IEEE paywall; uses TTS only as data augmentation tool

Title: Is it possible to train a Turkish TTS with English data?
Notes: Proof-of-concept only; no formal evaluation; authors describe output as sounding like an English tourist speaking Turkish

Title: Multilingual TTS for Turkic Languages Using Transliteration
Notes: Zero-shot from Kazakh; Turkish MOS only 3.25 (fair); 91% comprehensibility, 61% intelligibility; 18 Turkish raters

Title: Building a Turkish TTS Engine
Notes: Best MOS for Turkish TTS; largest Turkish dataset (63h); completely closed proprietary system; professional female voice actress

Title: Enhancing Quranic Learning: Arabic Phoneme Recognition
Notes: OUT OF SCOPE: excluded from the 19 surveyed works (screening record; not cited in the paper). Arabic phoneme classification, not TTS, not Turkish; authors are Turkish but work is unrelated

Title: Cross-Lingual Detection of Synthetic Speech
Notes: OUT OF SCOPE: excluded from the 19 surveyed works (screening record; not cited in the paper). Deepfake detection paper; no Turkish data tested despite Turkish authors

Title: Framework for Automatic Generation of Spoken QA Data
Notes: Uses Google Cloud TTS as black box; 223h is all synthetic; QA/NLP paper, not TTS

Title: End-to-End Generative System for Smart Travel Assistant
Notes: Industry demo (Yapi Kredi bank); MOS 2.98 is below acceptable; nothing released

Title: Direct Speech to Speech Turkish to Arabic
Notes: Corpus construction for speech translation; Turkish audio is noisy TV dialogue; Tacotron 2 discussed but never built

Title: Whisperer: Real-Time Prompting System
Notes: English-only teleprompter system; Turkish authors but no Turkish language content; uses Google TTS as black box

Title: A Novel End-to-End Turkish TTS System via Deep Learning
Model_Available: Partial (code only, no weights, no HiFi-GAN)
Notes: Best-documented open Turkish TTS; companion to P01; HiFi-GAN code missing from repo; text normalization uses English inflect library

Title: DUPLICATE of P02
Notes: Same paper as P02 (Karibayeva et al.)

Title: DUPLICATE of P07
Notes: Same DOI as P07 (Kocak & Buyukzincir)

Title: AI-Powered Turkish Speaking Robotic Personal Assistant
Notes: Uses lowest-quality TTS (pyttsx3/eSpeak); no evaluation; 4-page paper; rule-based NLP; Vosk ASR

Title: Mobile Reader: Turkish Scene Text Reader for Visually Impaired
Notes: Computer vision paper; TTS is off-the-shelf Android output; OCR contribution only

Title: AI-Based Mobile Banknote Recognition for Visually Impaired
Notes: Computer vision paper; TTS outputs single denomination word; MobileNetV2 95.56% accuracy

Title: Development of Turkish TTS Software for Visually Impaired (Okuyucu TTS)
Notes: 386 stored sound units; syllabification algorithm with 100% accuracy on 10K words; MOS 3.7; 10 visually impaired evaluators; Windows C#/.NET

Title: A Data Dictionary Set and Software Framework for Turkish TTS
Venue: Gazi Univ. Muh. Mim. Fak. Dergisi
Notes: 154 exceptional Turkish syllables cataloged; 11+ XML dictionaries; abbreviation/number handling; no audio output; framework never released

Title: DUPLICATE of P01
Notes: Same DOI as P01 (Cucen, Oyucu, Polat)

Title: A Corpus-Based Concatenative Speech Synthesis System for Turkish
Venue: Turkish J. Elec. Eng. Comp. Sci.
Notes: Earliest peer-reviewed Turkish TTS (Bogazici); highest concatenative MOS (4.2); corpus and software never distributed. Promoted from references R01 to reconcile body and catalog.

Title: Recommendations for Increasing the Naturalness in Turkish Text-to-Speech Synthesis
Notes: Pre-neural naturalness recommendations (pause modeling, prosodic stress, intonation); no finished system or corpus. Yilmaz-group lineage. Promoted from references R02.

Title: A Rule Based Prosody Model for Turkish Text-to-Speech Synthesis
Notes: Pre-neural rule-based prosody targets; no runnable system or eval corpus; with Uslu 2011 forms the most complete pre-neural Turkish pipeline. Promoted from references R03.
